posts.name: Dan Hammer
posts.cooked:
I just read the article in the NY Times about them, and downloaded the restaurant wine list.

Hawksmoor

Hawksmoor Steakhouse and Cocktail Bar

Award-winning New York steak restaurant and cocktail bar, between Madison Square Park and Gramercy Park, serving dry-aged steak and sustainably sourced seafood.

Est. reading time: 1 minute

I like their style regarding BYO, and you will too!

Click menus, then wine list, the download PDF. This is on page 2.

Wine Club Monday

On Mondays, we encourage you
to bring your own bottle of wine and
we’ll cork it for only $10.
There’s no tricky initiation test to join
the club, just come armed with a bottle
(or two or three…) and you’re in.
For maximum value bring a
Nebuchadnezzar of Champagne or a
Balthazar of Burgundy – there will still
only be a $10 corkage fee on each.
M

posts.name: Dan Hammer
posts.cooked: Here’s the article. https://www.nytimes.com/2021/12/21/dining/hawksmoor-steakhouse-review.html

posts.cooked:
I just read the article in the NY Times about them

Ooh thanks for this. I’ve read so much about Hawksmoor from the output of Davy Strange’s organ. Have got to go sometime

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: I remember taking advantage of their BYO policy in one of their London restaurants to celebrate finishing my undergraduate exams with a bottle of 1955 Latour!

posts.name: Mich@el Ch@ng
posts.cooked: I posted about this in the nyc restaurants thread, sounds nice

Unfortunately the food was terrible, with meat so salty as to be inedible.
But that aside, it would be nice if more restaurants had similar policies.

posts.name: Ramon Cabrera
posts.cooked: jp had broken the news and Shon posted a review at the Epicurean - NYC Restaurant thread.

posts.name: Y∅ñg £êë
posts.cooked: They are closed Sundays and Mondays. What am I missing here?

posts.name: Daniel. W i l l i g e r
posts.cooked: Too bad the food was not good. We have had consistently good steakhouse meals in Knightsbridge. And a very good Martini. Not a Connaught Martini but still quite nice!!
posts.display_username: Daniel. W i l l i g e r

posts.name: Steve Gautier
posts.cooked: We had a fantastic experience in London for our daughter’s LSE graduation dinner. Food was great and the service was excellent. Low key and not stuffy like some steak houses. BYO was a huge benefit on top of it.

posts.name: John Morris
posts.cooked:
I enjoyed meals in several of their London places, though the extra charges for sauce for your steak were amusing (something I see is carried over here).

With baked beets at $18, you may want to go for the corkage offer to keep the bill under control.

posts.name: M a r k K a p l a n
posts.display_username: M a r k K a p l a n

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
They are closed Sundays and Mondays. What am I missing here?

I think you are missing the opportunity to dine there on a Sunday or a Monday.

posts.name: Greg Kahn
posts.cooked:
Unfortunately the food was terrible, with meat so salty as to be inedible.
But that aside, it would be nice if more restaurants had similar policies.

Yeah, that’s what I’ve heard, sadly. As nice as low corkage policies are, most good NYC restaurants will let you bring your own wine with (an admittedly higher) corkage fee, and good food is a pretty important part of a restaurant experience for me

posts.name: M. Ch@o
posts.cooked:
Unfortunately the food was terrible, with meat so salty as to be inedible.
But that aside, it would be nice if more restaurants had similar policies.

Yeah, that’s what I’ve heard, sadly. As nice as low corkage policies are, most good NYC restaurants will let you bring your own wine with (an admittedly higher) corkage fee, and good food is a pretty important part of a restaurant experience for me

I’ve actually enjoyed the food (to be clear, in NY) on a couple of visits. As someone who usually does not need too much salt, I didn’t think it was overly salty. Get the bone marrow oysters, ribeye (no sauce) and fries—all you need.

posts.name: Russell Faulkner
posts.cooked: But it’s not really a British restaurant. It’s a British take on an American steakhouse.

posts.name: Rodrigo Br@g@
posts.cooked:
They are closed Sundays and Mondays. What am I missing here?

As I understand it, they haven’t yet opened service for Sunday and Monday. Likely a staffing issue. Unsure as to when they’ll open for those days. Anyone have any intel on that front?

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
I remember taking advantage of their BYO policy in one of their London restaurants to celebrate finishing my undergraduate exams with a bottle of 1955 Latour!

Of course you would . . . .

I think most of us were drinking beer and Crown Royal. Not mixed, though.
